message:
fix(security): fail-closed on NaN/Inf error_rate in VEF degraded mode engine

Add is_finite() guards to ProofLagSlo::breached_by() and
breaching_field() so that NaN, Infinity, and NEG_INFINITY error_rate
values are treated as SLO breaches rather than silently passing the
comparison (NaN < threshold is always false, meaning NaN would slip
through uncaught).

This is a defense-in-depth measure: upstream currently produces finite
values, but a corrupted metric source or division-by-zero in telemetry
aggregation could produce non-finite values. The fail-closed behavior
ensures the engine escalates to degraded/halt mode rather than
continuing to operate with poisoned metrics.

Also update the escalation diagnostic in VefDegradedModeEngine to
report the non-finite error_rate correctly.

Tests (+64 lines):
- nan_error_rate_in_metrics_breaches_slo
- inf_error_rate_in_metrics_breaches_slo
- neg_inf_error_rate_in_metrics_breaches_slo
- nan_error_rate_escalates_engine_to_halt

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
